                                                            [d/Tracker] = "#",

                                                            [d/Dataset] = "DS1",



                                                            //DS1 area is commented, as these values will be added to DATA()

                                                            //[d/DS_1_dim_join] = "#",

                                                            //[d/DS_1_dim1] = "#",

                                                            //[d/DS_1_dim2] = "#",

                                                            //[d/DS_1_dim3] = "#",



                                                            //DS2 area should be uniqely defined for JOIN to happen

                                                            [d/DS_2_dim_join] = "#",

                                                            [d/DS_2_dim1] = "#",

                                                            [d/DS_2_dim2] = "#",

                                                            [d/DS_2_dim3] = "#"

                                                           )

//this lookup is required to proceed JOIN on existing DS2 transactions only

* (RESULTLOOKUP(

                                                                                   [d/Tracker] = "Step1",

                                                                                   [d/Dataset] = "DS2",



                                                                                   //DS1 area should be uniqely defined

                                                                                   //[d/DS_1_dim_join] = "#", //this is commented as this is JOIN condition

                                                                                   [d/DS_1_dim1] = "#",

                                                                                   [d/DS_1_dim2] = "#",

                                                                                   [d/DS_1_dim3] = "#"



                                                                                   //DS2 area is commented to point lookup to DS2 Transactions

                                                                                   //[d/DS_2_dim_join] = "#",

                                                                                   //[d/DS_2_dim1] = "#",

                                                                                   //[d/DS_2_dim2] = "#",

                                                                                   //[d/DS_2_dim3] = "#"                      

                                                              ) * 0 + 1)



//also add DS2 measures

DELETE([d/Tracker] = "Step3")

DATA([d/Tracker] = "Step3", [d/Dataset] = "DS2") =

(RESULTLOOKUP([d/Tracker] = "Step2", [d/Dataset] = "DS2") * 0 + 1)



//this lookup is required to proceed JOIN on existing DS2 transactions only

* RESULTLOOKUP(

                                                              [d/Tracker] = "Step1",

                                                              [d/Dataset] = "DS2",



                                                              //DS1 area should be uniqely defined

                                                              //[d/DS_1_dim_join] = "#", //this is commented as this is JOIN condition

                                                              [d/DS_1_dim1] = "#",

                                                              [d/DS_1_dim2] = "#",

                                                              [d/DS_1_dim3] = "#"



                                                              //DS2 area is commented to point lookup to DS2 Transactions

                                                              //[d/DS_2_dim_join] = "#",

                                                              //[d/DS_2_dim1] = "#",

                                                              //[d/DS_2_dim2] = "#",

                                                              //[d/DS_2_dim3] = "#"                                                                    

                                                             )



//sum up all to same transactions to Result

DATA([d/Tracker] = "Result") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Tracker] = "Step2") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Tracker] = "Step3")



//remove Zeros on Res

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "DS_1_Amount", [d/Tracker] = "Result") = 0 OR RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "DS_2_Amount", [d/Tracker] = "Result") = 0 THEN

            DELETE([d/Tracker] = "Result")

ENDIF

 

 

 

 

 

This is how it looks in the DA trace run after execution:

 

In step 1 we added the join condition column to DS2 from File, step 2 is the join result with DS1 measure, step 3 is the join result with DS2 measure, and 'Result" member of the Tracker represents the final result

4. Create a story with the cross tab to see the initial step and the results:

This is how it looks in the story before execution

 

This is how it looks in the story after execution (intermediate steps were filtered out):

On the story side I have added another cross tab to show the File dataset data separately and made this planning enabled - if we need to cancel some of the join rules we can input Zero in the counter measure of this table and re-start the Data action. Changes after putting Zero on J11-J21 rule:

To conclude this scenario:

  • You can perform “joins” of sets of data in SAC
  • This requires advanced scripting
  • The principle of the script is
    • Use tracker dimension with new member each time you use DATA() to keep track of processing
    • restrict the columns to be added after join as # in the first RESULTLOOKUP() but keep them unrestricted in the second RESULTLOOKUP() of the statement.
    • in the same time keep dimensions responsible for the join condition unrestricted in both RESULTLOOKUP()’s
    • use the second RESULTLOOKUP() with *0+1 to go through existing transactions only and to avoid generation of new combinations or SAC errors related.

Have Fun!

entry.title: Hands-on use cases in SAC Planning Part 3: Front End Scripts to manage Master Data, Multi Actions
entry.content:
Intro

This is a series of 3 parts, you can find here Part 1 and Part 2

 

Manual Categorisation of Master Data

In many scenarios some of the incoming data requires categorisation to simplify the next steps of the business process – be it a planning scenario, or some other calculation where the rules can be defined by “categories” and not on the detailed level of the dimension values from incoming transactions. If at the same time this categorisation is artificial and cannot be derived from any source but manual input, this easily becomes a nightmare of endless local files and processes to keep them updated and to upload them to the reporting system in a reliable and consistent way. (missing old good Data Services with ‘wait for file’ and .bat features in the new BTP world)

SAC offers a wide range of API allowing to work with objects on the front end of a story, and this makes it possible to configure the process to update properties of a dimension from the story end (I will call the property an attribute as in the BW world). Below is the example I worked out after some research of SACAD1 Guide and some Community Blogs like this one. In my example I pre-filter the dimension values from the transactions filtered by Year, allowing end user to visually check members and identify the ones with property/attribute missing or requiring a new property/attribute value (category). My approach here is to update attributes values one by one, so 2 drop down objects are used to identify Dimension value and Attribute Value. After clicking the button corresponding Attribute is updated in the selected dimension value. To reproduce the example you need to:

  1. Create a Model with the Country dimension that has some members and CountryGroup property, and the CountryGroup dimension with some members (we will read available members from here).
  2. Input some data (simulating that there is some transactional data)
  3. Create a Story and add to canvas: Table, 2 dropdowns and a Button. Show properties of the Country dimension in the Table, don’t use ‘Unbooked’ option
  4. Turn on advanced mode of the Story
  5. Add Planning Model as a scripting object
  6. Add 3 following Scripting Variables of type PlanningModelMember: Member, SelMemberArray, TechMemberArray. Set as Array for *Array variables.
  7. Add 3 scripts – onInitialization script for the Page of the story, onResultChange of the Table and onClick for the Button object

Application will add members from the model dimensions to the dropdowns, execute update of the dimensions property after button click, refresh table content to reflect the latest state of the properties.

onInitialization:

 

 

 

Application.showBusyIndicator();

TechMemberArray = MyModel.getMembers("CountryGroup"); //Read all 

for(var k = 0; k < TechMemberArray.length; k++) 
	{		
		DD_DimProp.addItem(TechMemberArray[k].id); 	//add to Dropdown
	};
DD_DimProp.setSelectedKey('#');

var DS1 = MyTab.getDataSource();//read table content from page output
var Sel = DS1.getDataSelections({[Alias.MeasureDimension]: "Am"}); //Read all selections from table
for(k = 0; k < Sel.length; k++){
	var Memb = DS1.getResultMember("Country", Sel[k]);//point to parent dim in each Data Cell and add to Dropdown
	DD_ParentDim.addItem(Memb.id);		
} 

DD_ParentDim.setSelectedKey('#');
DD_DimProp.setSelectedKey('#');

Application.hideBusyIndicator();

 

 

 

 

 

 

OnResultChange:

 

 

 

var DS1 = MyTab.getDataSource();//read table content from page output
var Sel = DS1.getDataSelections({[Alias.MeasureDimension]: "Am"}); //Read all selections from table

DD_ParentDim.removeAllItems();//clean up and fill when table res changed
for(var k = 0; k < Sel.length; k++){
	var Memb = DS1.getResultMember("Country", Sel[k]);//point to parent dim in each Data Cell and add to Dropdown
	DD_ParentDim.addItem(Memb.id);		
} 

DD_ParentDim.setSelectedKey('#');
DD_DimProp.setSelectedKey('#');

 

 

 

 

OnClick:

 

 

 

Application.showBusyIndicator();
var ParentDim = DD_ParentDim.getSelectedKey();
var DimProp = DD_DimProp.getSelectedKey();

Member = ({ id: ParentDim,
					 properties: { CountryGroup: DimProp //Use Attribute name here as  { <Attribute Name>: DimProp	 						 
								 }}); 
SelMemberArray.push(Member); //Collect data for all members of ParentDim		
MyModel.updateMembers( "Country", Member );	//Use Dimension name here as MyModel.updateMembers( "<Dimension Name>", Member );		

DD_ParentDim.setSelectedKey('#');
DD_DimProp.setSelectedKey('#');

MyTab.getDataSource().refreshData();
Application.hideBusyIndicator();

 

 

 

 

Initial state in the story:

Selecting values in the DropDown:

After clicking the Button:

If you have a lot of values you can type and search, it works in dropdowns by default!

 

Managing prompt for Date dimension in Multi-Actions

This is something which pops-up when you try to work with different Models within single Multi-Action – Date Dimension can’t be used as a Cross-Model parameter:

 

As a result of this user needs to input the same Date values in all the Date parameters created for each Model used in the Action.

 

This is very much needed feature, to my mind, here you can find the initiative about this submitted some time ago – please vote!

I think we could avoid using different Models so that we don’t need this feature, but there is the other side of this scenario – f.e. Advanced Formulas require unambiguously definition in the scripts in the resultlookup’s quite often, and if we keep differently structured data within the same model we easily reach very big amount of dimensions, so that your Advanced Formula scripts start looking ridiculous (and it is not only about AF scripts in this regard).

Meanwhile my alternative in the front-end scripting for this is below:

1. Define your MultiAction in the scripting objects

 

2. Create a button or other visual object with OnClick script

 

 

 

 

 

Application.showBusyIndicator("Execution is in progress..."); 
var DS1 = Chart_5.getDataSource();//read content from the hidden chart
console.log(DS1);
if ( DS1.isResultEmpty() ){
			Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "There is no data available for selected time period") ; 
			}	
else {
	var Sel = DS1.getDataSelections({[Alias.MeasureDimension]: "Value"}); //Read all selections from the hidden chart
	var Memb = DS1.getResultMember("Date", Sel[0]);//read the 1st element of an array from the hidden chart for Date dimension
	//console.log(Sel);
	//console.log(Memb.id);
	MyMA.setParameterValue("DateYMD","[Date].[YMD].[Date.YEAR].[" + Memb.description +"]");
	MyMA.setParameterValue("DateHC", Memb.id);
	MyMA.setParameterValue("Date1", Memb.id);
	MyMA.setParameterValue("Date2", Memb.id);
	MyMA.setParameterValue("Date3", Memb.id);
	MyMA.setParameterValue("Date4", Memb.id);
	MyMA.setParameterValue("Date5", Memb.id);
	MyMA.setParameterValue("Date6", Memb.id);	
	MyMA.setParameterValue("Date7", Memb.id);
	MyMA.setParameterValue("Date8", Memb.id);	
		
	var DateYMD = MyMA.getParameterValue("DateYMD");
	console.log(DateYMD);

		var v_MA = MyMA.execute();
		console.log(v_MA.status);

		if (v_MA.status === MultiActionExecutionResponseStatus.Success){
			Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success, Memb.description + "Results were refreshed successfully.");
			} else {
					Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "An Error has occured trying to refresh " + Memb.description + " Results"); 
					}

	};	

Application.hideBusyIndicator();

 

 

 

3. As you can see in the comments, this script requires some hidden chart or table on the same page, from where we can derive the Date members to pass to the MA parameters. I have created a bar chart for this with the drill to the required Date granularity (Year in my example) and a filter using “Current Date Input Control” object . This would be easier to derive it directly from “Current Date Input Control” object, but I couldn’t find an appropriate API for this, moreover SAC APIs don’t work with Date Ranges if you work with ‘*.getDimensionFilters("Date")’. Alternative to this could be to Add a Date story filter with type range referenced to “Current Date Input Control” object and next to define a simple Input Control on the page to hide with Members type (not a range type), and if we use ‘Cascading Effect’ option on and ‘Booked’ members in the settings, this will contain only required members of the Date which are possible to save in the array and pass to the Multi Action in the script using ‘*.getActiveSelectedMembers()’, but I find it over-complicated and also I don’t want to filter all charts in my Story using Date global Story Filter, so I preferred a hidden chart.

4. Another important aspect of this script is that Date dimension can have different granularity in different Models, you can check the required value format used in ‘setParameterValue’ using ‘getParameterValue("DateYMD")’ and checking MA default parameters values in the browser console using ‘console.log("DateYMD")’. In my example all Date dimensions in the Models have YM hierarchy, but one Model has Date with YMD hierarchy, so I had to adapt the memb.id read from the hidden chart(which also works with YM hierarchy) and convert it to YMD using ‘setParameterValue("DateYMD","[Date].[YMD].[Date.YEAR].[" + Memb.description +"]")’

5. There are couple of IFs to check for errors and show the messages, so that we control the script behaviour and only customly defined errors may apper on the user screen.

6. I struggled a bit to create a custom button similar to Standard MultiAction starter, so I used SAP-font and Starter icon from here with the blue background, so it looks like this – 

to conclude: clicking the button will lead to execution of the Multi Action for the selected Year in "Current Date Input Control" object, passing the year value to all the date parameters

entry.title: Co-innovation program for SAP Customers
entry.content:
The SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC) is our co-innovation program that focuses on the continuous development and improvement of the SAP Enterprise Support services portfolio (Foundational Support) with a selected group of customers. 

Please find below our Q1/2025 ESAC workstreams update and an overview of all our workstreams running in Q1 here. Covering all major cloud solutions, at no additional fee.

New workstreams for Q1:

  • Continuous Quality Check (CQC): Deployment Readiness Check for SAP Analytics Cloud (see blog here)
  • Continuous Quality Check (CQC): Deployment Readiness for SAP Datasphere (see blog here)
  • Continuous Quality Check (CQC): Deployment Readiness for SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Expert Guided Implementation (EGI): Selective Data Transfer (SDT) to SAP Cloud ALM (see blog here)
  • New Service Booking App (SBA) in SAP4Me

We are also actively looking for more customers for the following open workstreams:

  • Continuous Quality Check (CQC): Deployment Readiness for SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite EXPANSION
  • Continuous Quality Check (CQC): Deployment Readiness for SAP Fieldglass
  • Continuous Quality Check (CQC): Reinvented Custom Code Analysis for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA, SAP ECC
  • Expert Guided Implementation (EGI): SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine and Generative AI (EMEA and APJ customers only)
  • Expert Guided Implementation (EGI): SAP Cloud Application Programming Development using SAP Build Code (EMEA and APJ customers only)
  • Service Results Dashboard for CQC services (improve look and feel of the Service Results dashboard)

To express interest, ask a question or join the program: contact us via esac@sap.com.

For more information you can also refer to our ESAC landing page on the SAP Support Portal.

Your SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council team

esac@sap.com

entry.title: Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q1 2025
entry.content:
Happy New Year All and we have a very exciting start to Q1 2025. We have so many great features coming this quarter it was hard to settle on a Top 5 but we have and in this blog we give you a sneak peek in to our Top 5 features in SAP Analytics Cloud for QRC1 2025. A full list of what is coming can be found on our Roadmap Explorer.

'Seamless planning' integration with SAP Datasphere

A much anticipated item for this quarter is the introduction of Seamless planning, a new approach to the planning integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere. With seamless planning, you can store the SAP Analytics Cloud model’s data (fact and master data) directly in SAP Datasphere, reducing the data footprint and making it available for real-time consumption, additional processing and extensibility scenarios using SAP Datasphere’s capabilities.

Main benefits include:

  • Real-time steering on semantically enriched data.
    Enhanced flexibility to perform live reporting across SAP Analytics Cloud and other data sources based on joint SAP Datasphere analytic models.
  • Extensibility of SAP Analytics Cloud planning using SAP Datasphere capabilities such as data operations using SQL editor.

Learn more in this great blog by @Max_Gander 

Compass – probability simulation

We are very excited to announce the arrival of SAP Analytics Cloud compass, which provides Monte Carlo simulation capabilities with a business user-friendly interface. This enables non-technical users to perform real-time multi-variate analysis without prior IT setup or advanced statistical skills.

With compass, we have broken down the barrier between this mathematical technique and business users by stripping away the complexity and providing an end-user centric interface for configuration and risk analysis. Now non-technical user can also enjoy the benefits of stochastic simulation without prior advanced maths knowledge or IT setup.

Find all the details of how compass works in this comprehensive blog by @ChenNee 

Video data story to present business insights using short videos

Another very exciting feature this quarter is the addition of the "Video Data Story". This feature will allow business users to design and create a short-form video directly inside SAP Analytics Cloud without any video editing expertise. It provides an alternative way to present the business insights using a brand-new format which could be more attractive and intuitive compared with the static content in story.

For step by step instructions on how to create one of these story check out this blog by @roy_jin 

Just ask enhancements

Just ask has been a popular feature in SAP Analytics Cloud and in this release we add several new features including:

  • Just Ask Support for Currency Conversion in Acquired Data Models
  • Just Ask now displays a limited number of data points in the bar charts. Unless you specify a rank or sort, the number of bars shown in a bar chart is limited to 10 bars.
  • Leveraging the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine for word embeddings and perform similarity queries

Job Monitor – Multi Action support

With the addition of Job Monitor, it will be much easier for business users and admin users to gain insights about the status of background jobs and overall processes.

You can now monitor the jobs created while transporting the content. All jobs related to transport operations (such as Export, Import, Download, and Upload) are listed in the Monitor page. You can quickly review the import summary from the Status column.

Learn more in this blog by @SarauShen 

There are plenty more exciting deliverables coming your way this quarter and we will be taking a deeper dive into these items in our What’s New video and blog series so stay tuned. For a full list of all features coming in QRC1 2025 for SAP Analytics Cloud check out the Roadmap Explorer and the What's New Viewer

Want to experience new and existing features for yourself? Take the leap and start your journey towards making data-driven decisions with confidence by signing up for a free trial, today.

#sap #sapanalytics

entry.title: New Cloud Connector IP ranges will be added January 19th 2025.
entry.content:
BACKGROUND

Cloud Connector is scheduled to update their IP ranges January 19th 2025. 

 

WHO THIS IMPACTS

  • Your organization uses SAP Cloud Connector 
    and
  • Your organization has firewall rules with an allowlist or whitelist for IP addresses

DETAILS

New SAP Cloud Connector IP addresses will be added January 19th 2025. If your organization has firewall rules that restrict traffic based on IP addresses, this should be updated as soon as possible.

SAP Cloud Connector is used extensively by SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere.

The link to the full list of IP addresses is here: https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/prerequisites#loioe23f776e4d594fdbaeeb1196d47bbcc0__cf
